Risk Description: Because of repeated defect reopenings (reopen count 0-2 per defect) and multiple retesting cycles (2-3 cycles per defect) documented in JIRA and DSR across Sprint 1-8, and confirmed by Finance Report showing QA budget overrun of ₹25,000, there is a risk that QA retesting effort will continue to exceed planned capacity, which could lead to further budget overruns, delayed sprint closures, and resource burnout.
Potential Impact: Financial loss of ₹25,000+ already incurred; continued overrun may impact project profitability and delay delivery timelines by 5-7 days per sprint.

Risk Description: Because of recurring environment access permissions, UAT/prod-like access blocks, PR approval delays (documented in DSR, WSR, and JIRA as 'Blocked' status across 20+ tasks), and certificate renewal delays (Sprint 5, Day 3-4), and corroborated by Metrics Report showing elevated cycle time of 5-6 days, there is a risk that dependency resolution timelines will continue to slip, which could lead to sprint spillovers, SLA breaches, and delayed go-live.
Potential Impact: Cycle time increase from 3-4 days to 5-6 days; potential SLA breach; delayed go-live by 1-2 weeks if dependencies remain unresolved.

Risk Description: Because of PR approval delays documented in DSR (all 8 sprints), WSR (Weeks 1-8), and JIRA (multiple 'Blocked' statuses for bug/task items awaiting PR approval), and confirmed by Metrics Report noting 'Approval delays observed' impacting velocity, there is a risk that development cycle time will remain elevated, which could lead to sprint velocity degradation, delayed defect fixes, and increased technical debt.
